rationale: Low spatial resolution and explicit time stepping limit both accuracy and stability, especially for this high-Peclet number problem; the plan is efficient but not robust or accurate enough for convection-dominated regimes.
Implicit Crank-Nicolson provides good stability for the stiff diffusion term and moderate accuracy, but central differencing is not ideal for high-Peclet advection; efficiency is reasonable.
High resolution and MUSCL with van Leer limiter are good for advection, but explicit time stepping is very restrictive for stability and computationally expensive for this problem.
Spectral accuracy and periodic BCs are a strong match, but explicit time stepping is limited by stability for high Peclet numbers; efficiency is moderate.

High-resolution Fourier spectral with IMEX time stepping is nearly optimal for this periodic, convection-diffusion problem: it combines high accuracy, excellent stability, and reasonable efficiency for the accuracy achieved.
Implicit time stepping is robust, but low-order FEM and moderate resolution limit accuracy; efficiency is good, but not ideal for periodic, smooth problems.
IMEX BDF2 and higher-order FEM improve stability and accuracy, but FEM is less efficient for periodic, smooth solutions compared to spectral methods.
Upwind scheme is appropriate for advection, and high resolution helps accuracy, but explicit time stepping is very costly and restrictive for stability in this regime.
Implicit time stepping is robust, but low-order FV limits accuracy; Godunov flux is stable but not as accurate for smooth, periodic solutions.
Semi-implicit (IMEX) approach is well-suited for convection-diffusion, providing good stability and moderate accuracy at reasonable cost, though not as accurate as spectral IMEX.
